stages.name: Prevention (P1-P2)
stages.position_description: Mutagenic damage accumulates. Lifestyle, chemoprevention, and screening operate here. The tumour has not yet committed.

stages.name: Interception (P3)
stages.position_description: A precancerous lesion is visible but reversible. Chemoprevention (aspirin in CRC, tamoxifen in breast) targets this window. The tumour is previewing its form — intervene before commitment.

stages.name: Early surgery (P4)
stages.position_description: The commitment threshold. Once crossed, the tumour is irreversibly on its path. Surgery at this position removes the committed structure before it builds. The most cost-effective intervention point.

stages.name: Neoadjuvant (P5-P8)
stages.position_description: The tumour is building its microenvironment. Neoadjuvant therapy disrupts the architecture before it encounters the wider body. The tumour is vulnerable because its structure is incomplete.

stages.name: Stage III Adjuvant
stages.position_description: Tumour surgically removed. Micro-metastases may remain. The system is building but not yet encountering.
stages.position_note: Tumour removed. Micro-metastases differentiating. Intervention targets residual cells before they establish.

stages.name: Stage IV 1L
stages.position_description: Metastases detected. The tumour has crossed into Encounter — it is now interacting with distant environments.
stages.position_note: Established metastatic disease. Tumour maintaining itself through active surveillance of its microenvironment.

stages.name: Stage IV 2L
stages.position_description: First-line failed. The tumour has integrated information from the first treatment and adapted.
stages.position_note: Resistant to first-line. Tumour compensating — drift has produced adapted subclones.

stages.name: Stage IV 3L+
stages.position_description: Multiple prior lines. The tumour is in conservation — actively maintaining resistance through surveillance and compensation loops.
stages.position_note: Multiply resistant. Tumour at cycle boundary — conservation exhausted, approaching next transformation.

param_descriptions.beta: Resistance adaptation rate — how quickly the tumour learns to compensate for treatment. Higher β means faster adaptation (logarithmic maturity).
param_descriptions.gamma: Prior-line penalty — how much each previous treatment line reduces the next line's effectiveness. Higher γ means prior treatments have more impact.
param_descriptions.epsilon_top: Coverage independence — whether agents in different sub-phases act independently (ε=0), synergistically (ε>0), or with overlap (ε<0).
param_descriptions.epsilon_sub: Sub-phase independence — same as above but within a single sub-phase when multiple agents target it.
param_descriptions.doubling_time_days: Tumour volume doubling time in days. Faster growth (lower dt) means the tumour cycles through the geometry more quickly.

param_explanations.beta: Resistance adaptation rate. How fast the tumour learns to compensate for treatment over time. Higher β = faster resistance. Appears as β·ln(1+τ) in the formula.
param_explanations.gamma: Prior-line penalty. How much each previous treatment line reduces subsequent response. Higher γ = more cross-resistance. Appears as γ·p in the formula.
param_explanations.epsilon_top: Sub-phase independence. Measures whether drugs targeting different sub-phases work independently (ε=0), synergistically (ε>0), or have overlapping effects (ε<0). Determines σ_cascade.
param_explanations.epsilon_sub: Within-sub-phase independence. Same as ε_top but for multiple drugs targeting the SAME sub-phase.
param_explanations.doubling_time_days: Tumour volume doubling time in days. Faster-growing tumours (lower dt) respond differently to treatment timing.

stages.name: Prevention (P1-P2)
stages.position_description: Mutagenic damage accumulates. Lifestyle, chemoprevention, and screening operate here. The tumour has not yet committed.

stages.name: Interception (P3)
stages.position_description: A precancerous lesion is visible but reversible. Chemoprevention (aspirin in CRC, tamoxifen in breast) targets this window. The tumour is previewing its form — intervene before commitment.

stages.name: Early surgery (P4)
stages.position_description: The commitment threshold. Once crossed, the tumour is irreversibly on its path. Surgery at this position removes the committed structure before it builds. The most cost-effective intervention point.

stages.name: Neoadjuvant (P5-P8)
stages.position_description: The tumour is building its microenvironment. Neoadjuvant therapy disrupts the architecture before it encounters the wider body. The tumour is vulnerable because its structure is incomplete.

stages.name: Adjuvant
stages.position_description: Post-surgery adjuvant. ER+ tumour removed; hormonal therapy targets residual disease in the construction phase.
stages.position_note: Post-surgery. ER+ cells differentiating. Endocrine therapy prevents re-establishment.

stages.name: Metastatic 1L
stages.position_description: Metastatic disease detected. First encounter with systemic therapy.
stages.position_note: Metastatic. ER-driven growth maintained through hormonal surveillance loop.

stages.name: Metastatic 2L
stages.position_description: Progressed on first-line. Tumour has integrated resistance to endocrine ± CDK4/6 inhibition.
stages.position_note: CDK4/6i-resistant. ESR1 mutations or bypass pathways compensating.

stages.name: Metastatic 3L+
stages.position_description: Heavily pretreated. Conservation regime — tumour maintains multi-drug resistance.
stages.position_note: Multi-endocrine resistant. Approaching phenotype switch.

stages.name: Prevention (P1-P2)
stages.position_description: Mutagenic damage accumulates. Lifestyle, chemoprevention, and screening operate here. The tumour has not yet committed.

stages.name: Interception (P3)
stages.position_description: A precancerous lesion is visible but reversible. Chemoprevention (aspirin in CRC, tamoxifen in breast) targets this window. The tumour is previewing its form — intervene before commitment.

stages.name: Early surgery (P4)
stages.position_description: The commitment threshold. Once crossed, the tumour is irreversibly on its path. Surgery at this position removes the committed structure before it builds. The most cost-effective intervention point.

stages.name: Neoadjuvant
stages.position_description: Neoadjuvant: treating before surgery. The tumour is in early construction — vulnerable to disruption before it completes.
stages.position_note: Pre-surgical. Tumour still in construction — attacking before full establishment.

stages.name: Adjuvant
stages.position_description: Post-surgery adjuvant. Architecture established but encounter has not begun.
stages.position_note: Post-resection. Residual micro-metastases differentiating.

stages.name: Metastatic 1L
stages.position_description: Metastatic first-line. The tumour is producing metastases — it has entered Encounter.
stages.position_note: Established metastatic NSCLC. PD-L1/driver mutation status determines surveillance mechanism.

stages.name: Metastatic 2L
stages.position_description: Second-line. The tumour has integrated resistance to first-line IO ± chemo.
stages.position_note: Progressed on 1L. Resistance mutations (T790M, MET amp) compensating.

stages.name: Metastatic 3L+
stages.position_description: Third-line. Conservation regime — the tumour monitors and compensates for therapeutic pressure.
stages.position_note: Multiply pretreated. Conservation regime nearly exhausted.

stages.name: Prevention (P1-P2)
stages.position_description: Mutagenic damage accumulates. Lifestyle, chemoprevention, and screening operate here. The tumour has not yet committed.

stages.name: Interception (P3)
stages.position_description: A precancerous lesion is visible but reversible. Chemoprevention (aspirin in CRC, tamoxifen in breast) targets this window. The tumour is previewing its form — intervene before commitment.

stages.name: Early surgery (P4)
stages.position_description: The commitment threshold. Once crossed, the tumour is irreversibly on its path. Surgery at this position removes the committed structure before it builds. The most cost-effective intervention point.

stages.name: Neoadjuvant (P5-P8)
stages.position_description: The tumour is building its microenvironment. Neoadjuvant therapy disrupts the architecture before it encounters the wider body. The tumour is vulnerable because its structure is incomplete.

stages.name: Adjuvant
stages.position_description: Post-resection adjuvant. Pancreatic cancer in construction — stroma is dense, architecture established.
stages.position_note: Post-Whipple. Residual cells in stroma-dense microenvironment.

stages.name: Locally advanced
stages.position_description: Locally advanced, unresectable. The tumour has selected its configuration but has not yet metastasised.
stages.position_note: Locally advanced. Tumour established but not yet metastatic — early conservation.

stages.name: Metastatic 1L
stages.position_description: Metastatic first-line. The tumour has crossed into Encounter. Dense stroma limits drug penetration.
stages.position_note: Metastatic. Dense stroma provides surveillance shield. High resistance to intervention.

stages.name: Metastatic 2L
stages.position_description: Second-line. Gemcitabine or FOLFIRINOX resistance integrated.
stages.position_note: Second-line. Compensating against prior chemotherapy. Extremely resistant.

stages.name: Prevention (P1-P2)
stages.position_description: Mutagenic damage accumulates. Lifestyle, chemoprevention, and screening operate here. The tumour has not yet committed.

stages.name: Interception (P3)
stages.position_description: A precancerous lesion is visible but reversible. Chemoprevention (aspirin in CRC, tamoxifen in breast) targets this window. The tumour is previewing its form — intervene before commitment.

stages.name: Early surgery (P4)
stages.position_description: The commitment threshold. Once crossed, the tumour is irreversibly on its path. Surgery at this position removes the committed structure before it builds. The most cost-effective intervention point.

stages.name: Neoadjuvant (P5-P8)
stages.position_description: The tumour is building its microenvironment. Neoadjuvant therapy disrupts the architecture before it encounters the wider body. The tumour is vulnerable because its structure is incomplete.

stages.name: mHSPC
stages.position_description: Hormone-sensitive. The tumour is still being tested by androgen deprivation — it has not yet selected its resistant form.
stages.position_note: Hormone-sensitive. AR-dependent growth — still differentiating along hormonal axis.

stages.name: mCRPC 1L
stages.position_description: Castration-resistant first-line. The tumour has discovered how to grow without androgen — a new relational state.
stages.position_note: Castrate-resistant. AR pathway reactivated despite ADT — surveillance mechanism adapted.

stages.name: mCRPC 2L
stages.position_description: CRPC second-line. The tumour has reached equilibrium with one line of CRPC therapy.
stages.position_note: Second-line CRPC. AR splice variants, neuroendocrine differentiation compensating.

stages.name: mCRPC 3L+
stages.position_description: CRPC third-line+. Conservation — multi-mechanism resistance maintained.
stages.position_note: Heavily pretreated. Near cycle exhaustion. Taxane-resistant, AR-independent.

stages.name: Prevention (P1-P2)
stages.position_description: Mutagenic damage accumulates. Lifestyle, chemoprevention, and screening operate here. The tumour has not yet committed.

stages.name: Interception (P3)
stages.position_description: A precancerous lesion is visible but reversible. Chemoprevention (aspirin in CRC, tamoxifen in breast) targets this window. The tumour is previewing its form — intervene before commitment.

stages.name: Early surgery (P4)
stages.position_description: The commitment threshold. Once crossed, the tumour is irreversibly on its path. Surgery at this position removes the committed structure before it builds. The most cost-effective intervention point.

stages.name: Neoadjuvant (P5-P8)
stages.position_description: The tumour is building its microenvironment. Neoadjuvant therapy disrupts the architecture before it encounters the wider body. The tumour is vulnerable because its structure is incomplete.

stages.name: Metastatic 1L
stages.position_description: Metastatic first-line. RCC is highly vascular — the encounter with the host vasculature defines its biology.
stages.position_note: Treatment-naive metastatic. VHL-loss driven angiogenesis provides surveillance mechanism.

stages.name: Metastatic 2L
stages.position_description: Second-line. The tumour has integrated resistance to first-line IO+TKI.
stages.position_note: Post-TKI/IO. Alternative angiogenic pathways compensating (MET, AXL).

stages.name: Metastatic 3L
stages.position_description: Third-line+. Conservation — multiple resistance mechanisms active simultaneously.
stages.position_note: Multiply pretreated. Conservation regime near exhaustion.

stages.position_description: Mutagenic damage accumulates. Lifestyle, chemoprevention, and screening operate here. The tumour has not yet committed.

stages.name: Metastatic 1L
stages.position_description: Metastatic first-line. CRC metastases typically to liver — the tumour is encountering a new tissue environment.
stages.position_note: First-line metastatic. Wnt/RAS/BRAF pathways maintain proliferative surveillance.

stages.name: Metastatic 2L
stages.position_description: Second-line. Resistance to first-line chemo±targeted integrated.
stages.position_note: Second-line. RAS mutations and EGFR resistance compensating.

stages.name: Metastatic 3L+
stages.position_description: Third-line+. Late conservation — limited options, tumour maintains multi-drug resistance.
stages.position_note: Refractory. Near cycle boundary. Very limited treatment options.

breast_hr|fulv+palbo|met_2l.mechanism: CDK4/6 inhibition exposes hidden ER dependency

breast_hr|abema+fulv|met_2l.mechanism: Strong CDK4/6 Transform exposes hidden ER dependency

breast_hr|fulv+ribo|met_2l.mechanism: CDK4/6 inhibition exposes hidden ER dependency

breast_hr|alpe+fulv|met_2l.mechanism: PI3K inhibition exposes ER pathway dependency
